title: hazardia squarrosa
intro: Hazardia squarrosa, also known as Isocryris or greasewood, is a type of flowering shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hazardia squarrosa include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as supporting the immune system. Hazardia squarrosa is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in conjunction with other herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
